AboutCode.tip: Say hello in the public room and mention the project area you're exploring.

Accord Project.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Alaska.tip: Use GitHub Discussions for mentor communication and project queries.

AnkiDroid.tip: Join the Discord server and go to #ankidroid-gsoc channel for GSoC updates and mentor communication.

AOSSIE.tip: Join the AOSSIE Discord server and discuss ideas publicly in project-specific channels.

Apache Software Foundation.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

API Dash.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

ArduPilot.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

BRL-CAD.tip: BRL-CAD uses group mentorship. Contact via Zulip at https://brlcad.zulipchat.com or email devs@brlcad.org.

cBioPortal for Cancer Genomics.tip: Ask project-idea questions on the cBioPortal Public Slack or in the linked GSoC issues.

CCExtractor Development.tip: Carlos is the main contact for CCExtractor

Ceph.tip: Join Ceph Slack from https://ceph.io/en/community/ and reach out to @Afreen @Abhishek Desai @Dnyaneshwari Talwekar. For questions contact Vallari Agrawal at vallari.agrawal@ibm.com

mailingLists.label: CERN HSF GSoC Maintainers Mailing List

CERN-HSF.tip: Please avoid posting extended information about yourself in the chat channel and reserve this for the direct exchange with the mentors.

CGAL Project.tip: Email gsoc-cgal@inria.fr for GSoC queries. For general discussion join cgal-discuss@inria.fr. Ideas page at https://github.com/CGAL/cgal/wiki/Project-Ideas-for-GSOC-2026

checkstyle.tip: Join the Discord server at https://discord.gg/F6MkcwvZT8. Each project idea has its own Discord QnA channel with invite link on the ideas page. Mailing list: checkstyle-devel@googlegroups.com

CNCF.tip: Do not use direct email. Use the upstream issues listed in each project's description to engage with mentors.

CRIU.tip: Clone and build CRIU locally, and run a simple checkpoint/restore test before reaching out.

Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative (CD.tip: Read GSoC proposal writing guide and discuss your ideas with mentors before submitting a proposal

channels.label: Data for the Common Good contact Email
Data for the Common Good on X

Data for the Common Good.tip: To contribute to the Project take a look at its Contribution Guidance in the GSoC organisation page.

DBpedia.tip: Join the Discord dev channel or contact the Databus team via email.

Debian.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

DeepChem.tip: Join the DeepChem Discord server and introduce yourself before reaching out to mentors directly.

Django Software Foundation.tip: Post your draft proposal to the Django Forum Mentorship channel for feedback. Start contributing to Django before applying to become a known contributor.

dora-rs.tip: Review the GSoC ideas page, join the GitHub Discussions forum and Discord community, and engage with the dora-rs contributors before submitting a proposal.

FFmpeg.tip: Read the developer documentation and introduce yourself on the mailing list before applying.

FLARE.tip: Create a new topic in GitHub Discussions at https://github.com/mandiant/flare-gsoc/discussions and mention @mandiant/flare-gsoc. Subscribe to mailing list by emailing 'subscribe' to flare-external@google.com

FOSSology.tip: Join and say hi in the GSoC channel before DMing mentors.

Free and Open Source Silicon Foundation.org: Free and Open Source Silicon Foundation
Free and Open Source Silicon Foundation.tip: Contact mentors listed on the ideas page directly, or email gsoc@fossi-foundation.org for general GSoC queries.

FreeCAD.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Gambit: The package for computation in g.org: Gambit: The package for computation in game theory

Gemini CLI.tip: Engage with mentors and discuss project ideas directly in GitHub Discussions or Issues.

German Center for Open Source AI.org: German Center for Open Source AI

Global Alliance for Genomics and Health.org: Global Alliance for Genomics and Health

mailingLists.label: gcc@gcc.gnu.org — primary GSoC discussion (put 'GSoC' in subject)

GNU Compiler Collection (GCC).tip: Email gcc@gcc.gnu.org with 'GSoC' in the subject to introduce yourself and discuss your project idea. For gccrs (Rust frontend) projects, use the Zulip or #gccrust IRC channel on irc.oftc.net instead.

GNU Mailman.tip: Primary mentor communication happens through the Mailman Developers mailing list or #mailman IRC.

GNU Project.tip: Email summer-of-code@gnu.org to introduce yourself. Sub-project contacts: GnuCOBOL → gnucobol-dev@gnu.org; GNU Aris → bug-aris@gnu.org; Libcdio → libcdio-devel@gnu.org; GNU XaoS → github.com/xaos-project; GNU Boot → git.savannah.gnu.org/cgit/gnuboot.git.

gprMax.tip: Introduce yourself before starting chat on Zulip.

GRAME.tip: Join Discord at https://discord.gg/RzuFg6B8zA and introduce yourself in the #gsoc channel

HumanAI.tip: Contact admins at human-ai@cern.ch or join the Gitter/Matrix channel at https://matrix.to/#/#humanai-foundation:gitter.im

Internet Archive.tip: Mentors collaborate via Slack. Use the interest form on the ideas page to connect with them before submitting your proposal.

IOOS.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

JabRef e.V..tip: Introduce yourself on the JabRef Discourse forum (https://discourse.jabref.org/) and mention the project idea you're interested in. Engage publicly rather than via DMs — mentors prefer transparent discussions. Contributing to 'good first issues' on GitHub is strongly encouraged before applying.

JBoss Community.tip: Channels are about generic doubts. For project-specific doubts you will need to contact project mentors and channels specified in the project description

Jenkins.tip: Join the Jenkins community channels and introduce yourself before asking project-specific questions.

Joomla!.tip: Use the Joomla! Forum or mailing list to connect with mentors.

Joplin.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Konflux.tip: Join and say hi in the GSoC channel before DMing mentors.

Kornia.tip: Join the Kornia Slack and contact mentors before proposal submission.

LibreCube Initiative.tip: Please email directly to the mentors (with gsoc@librecube.org in copy), with a short text about yourself (your skills and interests) and what of the project(s) you find interesting to work on.

LibreHealth.tip: Project ideas are discussed and updated on the LibreHealth Forum. Engage with the community early and seek feedback on your proposal ideas.

LibreOffice.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

libssh.tip: Join the libssh mailing list and interact before applying. A non-trivial merge request on GitLab is required before proposals are considered.

Liquid Galaxy project.tip: Visit the contributor guidance page at https://www.liquidgalaxy.eu/2025/11/GSoC2026.html and use the listed public channels there before contacting mentors directly.

LLVM Compiler Infrastructure.tip: To contribute to the Project take a look at its Contribution Guide

MariaDB.tip: Join the Zulip community and subscribe to the maria-developers mailing list before asking project-specific questions.

MDAnalysis.tip: Review the MDAnalysis GSoC ideas page and engage with the community before submitting a proposal.

MetaBrainz Foundation Inc.tip: Join #metabrainz on IRC/Matrix/Discord. Post GSoC proposals on the forum. Do not contact mentors privately.

MetaCall.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Mixxx.tip: Post a new topic in the GSoC stream with your background and interest.

MoFA Org.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Neutralinojs.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

NixOS Foundation.tip: Say hello in the public room and mention the project area you're exploring.

NumFOCUS.tip: NumFOCUS is an umbrella organization. For project-specific questions, contact the individual project communities under the umbrella.

omegaUp.tip: Join the Discord server to get help and connect with mentors. Make sure to solve at least 2 of 3 problems on the GSoC 2026 arena test.

Open Robotics.tip: Join the Open Robotics Zulip community, introduce yourself, and discuss your interests publicly before sending mentor inquiries.

Open Science Initiative for Perfusion Im.org: Open Science Initiative for Perfusion Imaging

Open Technologies Alliance - GFOSS.tip: Introduce yourself on the public mailing list before asking project-specific questions or submitting proposals.

OpenAFS.tip: Join the #openafs IRC channel on Libera.Chat or subscribe to the openafs-devel mailing list to reach mentors and discuss project proposals.

OpenAstronomy.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

channels.label: OpenVINO Toolkit Google Summer of Code Discussions

Oppia Foundation.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

OSGeo (Open Source Geospatial Foundation.tip: Post in the OSGeo Discourse community to introduce yourself and ask questions related to GSoC.

OWASP Foundation.tip: Join OWASP Slack via the invite link and post in the #gsoc channel. Each project has its own Slack channel listed on the ideas page.

PEcAn Project.tip: Slack signup is disabled — email kooper@illinois.edu for an invite.

Pharo Consortium.tip: Join Pharo Discord and go to the #gsoc-students channel to introduce yourself and discuss project ideas with mentors.

PostgreSQL.tip: Join the #gsoc-contributors channel on PostgreSQL Slack. Email gsoc-mentors@lists.postgresql.org before submitting your proposal.

preCICE.tip: Post in the GSoC category on the preCICE Discourse forum to introduce yourself and ask questions.

Python Software Foundation.tip: Say hello in the public room and mention the project area you're exploring.

QEMU.tip: Join #qemu-gsoc on OFTC IRC and introduce yourself before asking project-specific questions.

RTEMS Project.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

Ruby.tip: Blog: https://rubygsoc.github.io/ | For direct contact, email Saroj at saroj@zoras.me

SageMath.tip: Send a short introduction with your background and the project idea you're interested in.

St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.tip: Email clay.mcleod@stjude.org and stephanie.sandor@stjude.org with [GSoC] in the subject line. St. Jude works on Rust-based bioinformatics tooling — Sprocket (WDL workflow engine), omics (genomics primitives), and crankshaft (execution engine). Projects involve Rust, WDL, Python bindings (PyO3), SIMD, and genomic data structures.

stdlib.tip: Post a new topic in the GSoC stream with your background and interest.

Ste||ar group.tip: Join the Discord server and introduce yourself before asking project-specific questions.

Submitty.tip: Join the Submitty Zulip server to communicate with mentors and contributors.

Sugar Labs.tip: Join the Matrix space at #sugar-labs:matrix.org to introduce yourself before asking project-specific questions.

SW360.tip: Join and say hi in the GSoC channel before DMing mentors.

Swift.tip: Post in the Development & Tooling category with your GSoC proposal details.

SymPy.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

Synfig.tip: Check the forum for regular updates

The Honeynet Project.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

The JPF team.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

The Libreswan Project.tip: Send a short intro email with your background and the idea you're interested in.

The Linux Foundation.tip: Introduce yourself to the relevant Linux Foundation workgroup before applying and follow the contributor application template.

channels.label: NetBSD Foundation IRC Channel on Libera Chat

The ns-3 Network Simulator Project.tip: Contact developers through the mailing list or Zulip chat before refining proposals.

The OpenROAD Initiative.tip: Review OpenROAD-flow-scripts and contribute small fixes or tests before applying.

The P4 Language Consortium.tip: Post a new topic in the GSoC stream with your background and interest.

The Rust Foundation.tip: Post a new topic in the #gsoc channel on Rust Zulip (https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/421156-gsoc) with your background and project interest. GSoC org admins are Jakub Beránek (https://github.com/kobzol) and Jack Huey (https://github.com/jackh726). Discuss ideas publicly in the channel before DMing mentors.

Tiled.tip: Introduce yourself on the forum and mention your project interest.

Typelevel.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

UC OSPO.tip: Join the UC OSPO Network Slack and email ospo-info-group@ucsc.edu for any eligibility questions.

Unikraft.tip: Join the Unikraft Discord, make a small contribution, and discuss project ideas with mentors before applying.

United Nations Office of Information Com.org: United Nations Office of Information Communication Technology
United Nations Office of Information Com.tip: Contact mentors directly via email or reach org admins at mithusa.kajendran@un.org for general GSoC questions.

VideoLAN.tip: Join #videolan on Libera.Chat IRC or send an email to the mailing list to introduce yourself before asking project-specific questions.

Wagtail.tip: Use GitHub Discussions at github.com/wagtail/gsoc/discussions to introduce yourself and ask questions before contacting mentors directly.

webpack.tip: Introduce yourself in the public channel before asking project-specific questions.

Wikimedia Foundation.tip: Post a new topic in the GSoC stream with your background and interest.

Zulip.tip: Join the Zulip contributor chat and introduce yourself before discussing project ideas.
